55 namespace boost::corosio::detail {
56
57 class io_uring_tcp_service;
58 class io_uring_tcp_acceptor_service; // Task 18
59 class io_uring_local_stream_service;
60 class io_uring_local_stream_acceptor_service;
61 class io_uring_udp_service;
62 class io_uring_local_datagram_service;
63
64 /** TCP socket implementation for io_uring.
65
66 Implements `tcp_socket::implementation` using a proactor model:
67 read, write, and connect operations are submitted to the kernel
68 via `io_uring_submit_op` and complete through the ring's CQE path.
69
70 The object is always owned by a `shared_ptr` managed by the service.
71 In-flight ops hold an additional `shared_ptr` copy (`impl_ptr`) so
72 the kernel's user-data pointer remains valid until the CQE arrives.
73
74 @par Thread Safety
75 Distinct objects: Safe.
76 Shared objects: Unsafe. A socket must not have two operations of
77 the same type in flight simultaneously.
78 */
79 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_tcp_socket final
80 : public native_socket_base<
81 io_uring_tcp_socket, tcp_socket::implementation, endpoint>
82 {
83 friend io_uring_tcp_service;
84
85 int family_ = AF_UNSPEC; // cached at open_socket
86 io_uring_scheduler* sched_ = nullptr;
87 io_uring_tcp_service* svc_ = nullptr;
88
89 // fd_ and local_endpoint_ are provided by native_socket_base (the
90 // readiness/completion-agnostic socket base shared with the reactor
91 // sockets). native_handle()/is_open()/set_option()/get_option() come
92 // from there too; local_endpoint() is overridden below for lazy
93 // getsockname resolution.
94 // Three-state machine for the local endpoint:
95 // unresolved — never set; accessor returns default endpoint
96 // (open-but-unbound socket, failed-connect, etc.)
97 // lazy_pending — set by adopt_fd to signal "this socket has an
98 // authoritative local endpoint that hasn't been
99 // fetched yet"; accessor will getsockname on
100 // first read
101 // resolved — local_endpoint_ is authoritative; accessor
102 // returns the cached value
103 enum class endpoint_state : int { unresolved, lazy_pending, resolved };
104 mutable std::atomic<endpoint_state> local_endpoint_state_
105 { endpoint_state::unresolved };
106 endpoint remote_endpoint_;
107
108 // Per-fd op slots — embedded to eliminate per-call heap allocation.
109 // Single-pending invariant per slot: at most one read, write, or
110 // connect in flight on this socket at any time (the awaitable
111 // contract).
112 uring_read_op rd_;
113 uring_write_op wr_;
114 uring_connect_op conn_;
115 uring_wait_op wait_op_;
116
117 mutable detail::speculative_state spec_;
118
119 public:
120 /** Construct with service and scheduler references.
121
122 Both refs must outlive this socket. `sched_` and `svc_` are
123 intentionally separate so service subclasses can pass a
124 different scheduler if needed.
125
126 @param svc The owning service (Task 13).
127 @param sched The io_uring scheduler owned by the context.
128 */
129 13199x explicit io_uring_tcp_socket(
130 io_uring_tcp_service& svc,
131 io_uring_scheduler& sched) noexcept
132 26398x : sched_(&sched)
133 13199x , svc_(&svc)
134 13199x {}
135
136 13199x ~io_uring_tcp_socket() override
137 13199x {
138 13199x if (fd_ >= 0)
139 ✗ ::close(fd_);
140 13199x }
141
142 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
143 // io_stream::implementation
144 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
145
146 190639x std::coroutine_handle<> read_some(
147 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
148 capy::executor_ref ex,
149 buffer_param buffers,
150 std::stop_token token,
151 std::error_code* ec,
152 std::size_t* bytes) override
153 {
154 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
155 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
156 190639x buffers.copy_to(
157 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
158 190639x io_uring_max_iov));
159 190639x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
160 190639x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
161
162 190639x ssize_t n = 0;
163 190639x int err = 0;
164 190639x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
165 190639x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_read())
166 {
167 190447x do { n = ::readv(fd_, iovecs, iovec_count); }
168 190447x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
169 190447x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
170 {
171 190428x have_sync_res = true;
172 190428x if (n < 0) err = errno;
173 // Speculative read produced a definitive answer (data
174 // or non-EAGAIN error); reset the failure streak so a
175 // burst of past EAGAINs doesn't latch perma-off when
176 // the workload is in fact speculation-friendly.
177 190428x if (n >= 0)
178 190428x spec_.on_read_success();
179 }
180 else
181 {
182 19x spec_.on_read_exhausted();
183 }
184 }
185
186 190639x if (have_sync_res)
187 {
188 190430x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
189 {
190 179231x decode_io_result(
191 ✗ ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
192 /*is_read=*/true,
193 179231x n < 0 ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n), empty_buf);
194 179231x if (bytes)
195 179231x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
196 179231x rd_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
197 179231x return dispatch_coro(ex, rd_.cont_op.cont);
198 }
199 11199x rd_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
200 22398x shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
201 11199x if (stop_now)
202 ✗ rd_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
203 else
204 11199x rd_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
205 11199x sched_->work_started();
206 {
207 11199x io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
208 11199x sched_->push_completed_locked(&rd_);
209 11199x }
210 11199x return std::noop_coroutine();
211 }
212
213 209x rd_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
214 418x shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
215 209x sched_->work_started();
216 209x if (rd_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
217 {
218 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
219 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&rd_);
220 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
221 ✗ }
222 209x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &rd_);
223 209x return std::noop_coroutine();
224 }
225
226 190564x std::coroutine_handle<> write_some(
227 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
228 capy::executor_ref ex,
229 buffer_param buffers,
230 std::stop_token token,
231 std::error_code* ec,
232 std::size_t* bytes) override
233 {
234 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
235 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
236 190564x buffers.copy_to(
237 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
238 190564x io_uring_max_iov));
239 190564x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
240 190564x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
241
242 190564x ssize_t n = 0;
243 190564x int err = 0;
244 190564x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
245 190564x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_write())
246 {
247 190562x msghdr msg{};
248 190562x msg.msg_iov = iovecs;
249 190562x msg.msg_iovlen = static_cast<decltype(msg.msg_iovlen)>(iovec_count);
250 190562x do { n = ::sendmsg(fd_, &msg, MSG_NOSIGNAL); }
251 190562x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
252 190562x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
253 {
254 190562x have_sync_res = true;
255 190562x if (n < 0) err = errno;
256 }
257 else
258 {
259 ✗ spec_.on_write_exhausted();
260 }
261 }
262
263 190564x if (have_sync_res)
264 {
265 190564x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
266 {
267 179364x decode_io_result(
268 1x ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
269 /*is_read=*/false, /*bytes=*/0, /*empty_buffer=*/false);
270 179363x if (bytes)
271 179363x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
272 179363x wr_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
273 179363x return dispatch_coro(ex, wr_.cont_op.cont);
274 }
275 11201x wr_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
276 22402x shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
277 11201x if (stop_now)
278 ✗ wr_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
279 else
280 11201x wr_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
281 11201x sched_->work_started();
282 {
283 11201x io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
284 11201x sched_->push_completed_locked(&wr_);
285 11201x }
286 11201x return std::noop_coroutine();
287 }
288
289 ✗ wr_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
290 ✗ shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
291 ✗ sched_->work_started();
292 ✗ if (wr_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
293 {
294 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
295 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&wr_);
296 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
297 ✗ }
298 ✗ io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &wr_);
299 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
300 }
301
302 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
303 // tcp_socket::implementation
304 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
305
306 4375x std::coroutine_handle<> connect(
307 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
308 capy::executor_ref ex,
309 endpoint ep,
310 std::stop_token token,
311 std::error_code* ec) override
312 {
313 4375x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
314 4375x if (stop_now)
315 {
316 1x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
317 {
318 1x if (ec) *ec = capy::error::canceled;
319 1x conn_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
320 1x return dispatch_coro(ex, conn_.cont_op.cont);
321 }
322 ✗ conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, family_, conn_.addr);
323 ✗ conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
324 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
325 ✗ conn_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
326 ✗ sched_->work_started();
327 {
328 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
329 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
330 ✗ }
331 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
332 }
333
334 // A speculative ::connect would leave the fd in EINPROGRESS and
335 // a subsequent IORING_OP_CONNECT would see EALREADY — avoid.
336 4374x conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, family_, conn_.addr);
337 4374x conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
338 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
339 4374x sched_->work_started();
340 4374x if (conn_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
341 {
342 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
343 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
344 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
345 ✗ }
346 4374x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &conn_);
347 4374x return std::noop_coroutine();
348 }
349
350 5x std::coroutine_handle<> wait(
351 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
352 capy::executor_ref ex,
353 wait_type w,
354 std::stop_token token,
355 std::error_code* ec) override
356 {
357 5x int poll_flags = 0;
358 5x switch (w)
359 {
360 3x case wait_type::read: poll_flags = POLLIN; break;
361 2x case wait_type::write: poll_flags = POLLOUT; break;
362 ✗ case wait_type::error: poll_flags = POLLPRI | POLLERR | POLLHUP; break;
363 }
364 5x wait_op_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_,
365 10x shared_from_this(), poll_flags, token);
366 5x sched_->work_started();
367 5x if (wait_op_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
368 {
369 1x io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
370 1x sched_->push_completed_locked(&wait_op_);
371 1x return std::noop_coroutine();
372 1x }
373 4x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &wait_op_);
374 4x return std::noop_coroutine();
375 }
376
377 3x std::error_code shutdown(tcp_socket::shutdown_type what) noexcept override
378 {
379 3x if (::shutdown(fd_, static_cast<int>(what)) != 0)
380 ✗ return make_err(errno);
381 3x return {};
382 }
383
384 // native_handle() / set_option() / get_option() are inherited from
385 // native_socket_base.
386
387 69x void cancel() noexcept override
388 {
389 69x if (fd_ >= 0)
390 69x sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(fd_);
391 69x }
392
393 /// Cancel in-flight ops, close the fd, and reset cached endpoints.
394 /// Called by the service on close()/teardown. cancel_and_flush submits
395 /// the cancel SQE while the fd is still open so IORING_ASYNC_CANCEL_FD
396 /// resolves before the fd number can be recycled.
397 21967x void close_socket() noexcept
398 {
399 21967x if (fd_ >= 0)
400 {
401 8768x sched_->cancel_and_flush(fd_);
402 8768x ::close(fd_);
403 8768x fd_ = -1;
404 }
405 21967x local_endpoint_ = endpoint{};
406 21967x remote_endpoint_ = endpoint{};
407 21967x local_endpoint_state_.store(
408 endpoint_state::unresolved, std::memory_order_release);
409 21967x }
410
411 26x endpoint local_endpoint() const noexcept override
412 {
413 // Lazy resolution: only fire the getsockname syscall when
414 // adopt_fd marked the endpoint as "lazy_pending". For
415 // unbound/disconnected sockets the state remains unresolved
416 // and the accessor returns the default endpoint without a
417 // syscall. The mutable update races benignly with concurrent
418 // readers — both threads would compute the same value from
419 // the same fd.
420 26x if (local_endpoint_state_.load(std::memory_order_acquire)
421 == endpoint_state::lazy_pending
422 26x && fd_ >= 0)
423 {
424 9x sockaddr_storage local{};
425 9x socklen_t len = sizeof(local);
426 18x if (::getsockname(
427 9x fd_,
428 9x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &len) == 0)
429 9x local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_endpoint(local);
430 9x local_endpoint_state_.store(
431 endpoint_state::resolved, std::memory_order_release);
432 }
433 26x return local_endpoint_;
434 }
435
436 25x endpoint remote_endpoint() const noexcept override
437 {
438 25x return remote_endpoint_;
439 }
440 };
441
442 /** TCP socket service for io_uring.
443
444 Owns all `io_uring_tcp_socket` implementations for an `io_context`.
445 Satisfies the `tcp_service` interface so the generic `tcp_socket`
446 front-end can call `open_socket` and `bind_socket` transparently.
447
448 Socket impls are reference-counted inside the service map; raw
449 pointers returned from `construct()` remain valid until `destroy()`
450 or `shutdown()` is called.
451
452 @par Thread Safety
453 All public member functions are thread-safe.
454 */
455 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_tcp_service final
456 : public io_uring_socket_service_base<
457 io_uring_tcp_service, tcp_service, io_uring_tcp_socket>
458 {
459 using base_service = io_uring_socket_service_base<
460 io_uring_tcp_service, tcp_service, io_uring_tcp_socket>;
461
462 public:
463 /// Identifies this service for `execution_context` lookup.
464 using key_type = tcp_service;
465
466 /** Construct the TCP service.
467
468 @param ctx The owning execution context. The io_uring scheduler
469 must already be registered.
470 */
471 551x explicit io_uring_tcp_service(capy::execution_context& ctx)
472 551x : base_service(ctx)
473 551x {}
474
475 // construct / destroy / shutdown / close / scheduler() are inherited
476 // from io_uring_socket_service_base. The methods below are TCP-specific.
477
478 /** Open a socket fd and associate it with an impl.
479
480 Creates a non-blocking, close-on-exec socket via `socket(2)`.
481
482 @param impl The socket implementation to initialise.
483 @param family Address family (e.g. `AF_INET`, `AF_INET6`).
484 @param type Socket type (e.g. `SOCK_STREAM`).
485 @param protocol Protocol number (e.g. `IPPROTO_TCP`).
486 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
487 */
488 4402x std::error_code open_socket(
489 tcp_socket::implementation& impl,
490 int family, int type, int protocol) override
491 {
492 4402x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_tcp_socket&>(impl);
493 4402x int fd = ::socket(
494 family, type | SOCK_NONBLOCK | SOCK_CLOEXEC, protocol);
495 4402x if (fd < 0)
496 ✗ return make_err(errno);
497 4402x if (sock.fd_ >= 0)
498 {
499 ✗ sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(sock.fd_);
500 ✗ ::close(sock.fd_);
501 }
502 4402x sock.fd_ = fd;
503 4402x sock.family_ = family;
504 // Mirror epoll/select: IPv6 sockets default to v6-only so they
505 // behave consistently across platforms regardless of the kernel
506 // default for /proc/sys/net/ipv6/bindv6only.
507 4402x if (family == AF_INET6)
508 {
509 8x int one = 1;
510 8x ::setsockopt(fd, IPPROTO_IPV6, IPV6_V6ONLY, &one, sizeof(one));
511 }
512 4402x return {};
513 }
514
515 /** Bind the socket and capture the local endpoint via `getsockname`.
516
517 @param impl The socket implementation to bind.
518 @param ep The local endpoint to bind to.
519 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
520 */
521 7x std::error_code bind_socket(
522 tcp_socket::implementation& impl, endpoint ep) override
523 {
524 7x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_tcp_socket&>(impl);
525 7x sockaddr_storage addr{};
526 7x socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(ep, addr);
527 7x if (::bind(
528 sock.fd_,
529 7x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), len) < 0)
530 2x return make_err(errno);
531
532 5x sockaddr_storage local{};
533 5x socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
534 5x if (::getsockname(
535 sock.fd_,
536 5x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
537 5x sock.local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_endpoint(local);
538 5x sock.local_endpoint_state_.store(
539 io_uring_tcp_socket::endpoint_state::resolved,
540 std::memory_order_release);
541 5x return {};
542 }
543
544 /** Wrap an already-accepted fd as a new socket impl.
545
546 Called by the acceptor service (Task 17) after `accept(2)`
547 returns a connected fd. Captures both endpoints via the provided
548 peer address and a `getsockname` call.
549
550 @param fd Accepted file descriptor (must be non-blocking).
551 @param peer Peer endpoint from `accept(2)`.
552 @return Raw pointer to the registered impl.
553 */
554 4366x io_uring_tcp_socket* adopt_fd(int fd, endpoint const& peer)
555 {
556 4366x auto p = std::make_shared<io_uring_tcp_socket>(*this, *sched_);
557 4366x p->fd_ = fd;
558 4366x p->remote_endpoint_ = peer;
559 // Mark the local endpoint as authoritative-but-unresolved.
560 // The accessor will fetch it via getsockname on first call.
561 // Accept-heavy workloads that never query the local endpoint
562 // skip the syscall entirely.
563 4366x p->local_endpoint_state_.store(
564 io_uring_tcp_socket::endpoint_state::lazy_pending,
565 std::memory_order_release);
566
567 8732x return this->register_impl(std::move(p));
568 4366x }
569 };
570
571 /** TCP acceptor implementation for io_uring.
572
573 Inherits the multishot machinery (parked-fd queue, waiter queue,
574 CQE drain on destruction) from `io_uring_multishot_acceptor_base`.
575 This class adds only the `accept()` override (matching
576 `tcp_acceptor::implementation`'s exact signature) and the
577 `adopt_thunk` static that wraps an accepted fd via
578 `io_uring_tcp_service::adopt_fd`.
579 */
580 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_tcp_acceptor final
581 : public io_uring_multishot_acceptor_base<
582 io_uring_tcp_acceptor,
583 tcp_acceptor::implementation,
584 endpoint,
585 io_uring_tcp_service>
586 {
587 friend io_uring_tcp_acceptor_service;
588
589 using base_type = io_uring_multishot_acceptor_base<
590 io_uring_tcp_acceptor,
591 tcp_acceptor::implementation,
592 endpoint,
593 io_uring_tcp_service>;
594
595 // Readiness-wait slot. The multishot accept op delivers accepted
596 // fds, but `wait()` reports raw poll readiness on the listening fd
597 // without consuming a connection — see the wait() override.
598 uring_wait_op wait_op_;
599
600 public:
601 121x explicit io_uring_tcp_acceptor(
602 io_uring_tcp_acceptor_service&,
603 io_uring_scheduler& sched,
604 io_uring_tcp_service& peer_svc) noexcept
605 121x : base_type(sched, peer_svc)
606 121x {}
607
608 4382x std::coroutine_handle<> accept(
609 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
610 capy::executor_ref ex,
611 std::stop_token token,
612 std::error_code* ec,
613 io_object::implementation** impl_out) override
614 {
615 4382x base_type::dispatch_or_queue(h, ex, token, ec, impl_out);
616 4382x return std::noop_coroutine();
617 }
618
619 2x std::coroutine_handle<> wait(
620 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
621 capy::executor_ref ex,
622 wait_type w,
623 std::stop_token token,
624 std::error_code* ec) override
625 {
626 2x int poll_flags = 0;
627 2x switch (w)
628 {
629 2x case wait_type::read: poll_flags = POLLIN; break;
630 ✗ case wait_type::write: poll_flags = POLLOUT; break;
631 ✗ case wait_type::error: poll_flags = POLLPRI | POLLERR | POLLHUP; break;
632 }
633 2x wait_op_.prepare(h, ex, ec, this->fd_, this->sched_,
634 4x this->shared_from_this(), poll_flags, token);
635 2x this->sched_->work_started();
636 2x if (wait_op_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
637 {
638 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(this->sched_->dispatch_mutex());
639 ✗ this->sched_->push_completed_locked(&wait_op_);
640 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
641 ✗ }
642 2x io_uring_submit_op(*this->sched_, &wait_op_);
643 2x return std::noop_coroutine();
644 }
645
646 4366x static io_object::implementation* adopt_thunk(
647 void* peer_service, int fd,
648 sockaddr_storage const& peer, socklen_t /*peer_len*/) noexcept
649 {
650 4366x auto* svc = static_cast<io_uring_tcp_service*>(peer_service);
651 4366x return svc->adopt_fd(fd, sockaddr_to_endpoint(peer));
652 }
653 };
654
655 /** TCP acceptor service for io_uring.
656
657 Owns all `io_uring_tcp_acceptor` implementations for an `io_context`.
658 Satisfies the `tcp_acceptor_service` interface so the generic
659 `tcp_acceptor` front-end can call `open_acceptor_socket`,
660 `bind_acceptor`, and `listen_acceptor` transparently.
661
662 Acceptor impls are reference-counted inside the service map; raw
663 pointers returned from `construct()` remain valid until `destroy()`
664 or `shutdown()` is called.
665
666 @par Thread Safety
667 All public member functions are thread-safe.
668 */
669 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_tcp_acceptor_service final
670 : public tcp_acceptor_service
671 {
672 public:
673 /// Identifies this service for `execution_context` lookup.
674 using key_type = tcp_acceptor_service;
675
676 /** Construct the TCP acceptor service.
677
678 @param ctx The owning execution context. Both the io_uring scheduler
679 and the TCP socket service must already be registered.
680 */
681 551x explicit io_uring_tcp_acceptor_service(capy::execution_context& ctx)
682 1653x : sched_(&ctx.use_service<io_uring_scheduler>())
683 551x , peer_svc_(&ctx.use_service<io_uring_tcp_service>())
684 551x {}
685
686 551x void shutdown() override
687 {
688 551x std::vector<std::shared_ptr<io_uring_tcp_acceptor>> live;
689 {
690 551x std::lock_guard lk(mutex_);
691 551x live.reserve(impls_.size());
692 551x for (auto& [_, p] : impls_)
693 ✗ live.push_back(p);
694 551x }
695 // Cancel without the lock held to avoid inversion if cancel()
696 // re-enters the service.
697 551x for (auto& p : live)
698 ✗ p->cancel();
699 551x }
700
701 121x io_object::implementation* construct() override
702 {
703 auto p = std::make_shared<io_uring_tcp_acceptor>(
704 121x *this, *sched_, *peer_svc_);
705 121x auto* raw = p.get();
706 121x std::lock_guard lk(mutex_);
707 121x impls_.emplace(raw, std::move(p));
708 121x return raw;
709 121x }
710
711 121x void destroy(io_object::implementation* p) override
712 {
713 121x if (!p)
714 ✗ return;
715 121x std::lock_guard lk(mutex_);
716 121x impls_.erase(static_cast<io_uring_tcp_acceptor*>(p));
717 121x }
718
719 // Close the fd eagerly when tcp_acceptor::close() is called, before
720 // destroy() drops the shared_ptr and the destructor runs.
721 236x void close(io_object::handle& h) override
722 {
723 236x auto* acc = static_cast<io_uring_tcp_acceptor*>(h.get());
724 236x if (acc && acc->fd_ >= 0)
725 {
726 // Flush the cancel SQE before closing the fd so the kernel
727 // resolves the file from the fd number while it is still
728 // valid. drain_waiters_only avoids submitting cancel-by-fd
729 // a second time (cancel_and_flush already did it).
730 115x sched_->cancel_and_flush(acc->fd_);
731 115x acc->drain_waiters_only();
732 115x ::close(acc->fd_);
733 115x acc->fd_ = -1;
734
735 // Break the multi_op_ -> impl_ptr (shared_ptr<this>) cycle
736 // start_multishot established. The acceptor destructor's
737 // drain_cqes_for(multi_op_.get()) is the safety net; here
738 // we just drop the cycle so the impl can be released when
739 // the user's last shared_ptr does.
740 115x if (acc->multi_op_)
741 101x acc->multi_op_->impl_ptr.reset();
742 }
743 236x }
744
745 /** Create a non-blocking, close-on-exec socket for accepting.
746
747 @param impl The acceptor implementation to initialise.
748 @param family Address family (e.g. `AF_INET`, `AF_INET6`).
749 @param type Socket type (e.g. `SOCK_STREAM`).
750 @param protocol Protocol number (e.g. `IPPROTO_TCP`).
751 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
752 */
753 115x std::error_code open_acceptor_socket(
754 tcp_acceptor::implementation& impl,
755 int family,
756 int type,
757 int protocol) override
758 {
759 115x auto& acc = static_cast<io_uring_tcp_acceptor&>(impl);
760 115x int fd = ::socket(
761 family, type | SOCK_NONBLOCK | SOCK_CLOEXEC, protocol);
762 115x if (fd < 0)
763 ✗ return make_err(errno);
764 115x if (acc.fd_ >= 0)
765 {
766 ✗ sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(acc.fd_);
767 ✗ ::close(acc.fd_);
768 }
769 115x acc.fd_ = fd;
770 // Match epoll/select: IPv6 acceptors default to dual-stack
771 // (v6-only=false) so they accept both IPv4 and IPv6 connections.
772 115x if (family == AF_INET6)
773 {
774 10x int zero = 0;
775 10x ::setsockopt(fd, IPPROTO_IPV6, IPV6_V6ONLY, &zero, sizeof(zero));
776 }
777 115x return {};
778 }
779
780 /** Bind an open acceptor and capture the local endpoint.
781
782 @param impl The acceptor implementation to bind.
783 @param ep The local endpoint to bind to.
784 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
785 */
786 113x std::error_code bind_acceptor(
787 tcp_acceptor::implementation& impl, endpoint ep) override
788 {
789 113x auto& acc = static_cast<io_uring_tcp_acceptor&>(impl);
790 113x sockaddr_storage addr{};
791 113x socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(ep, addr);
792 113x if (::bind(
793 acc.fd_,
794 113x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), len) < 0)
795 4x return make_err(errno);
796
797 109x sockaddr_storage local{};
798 109x socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
799 109x if (::getsockname(
800 acc.fd_,
801 109x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
802 109x acc.local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_endpoint(local);
803 109x return {};
804 }
805
806 /** Start listening and submit the multishot accept SQE.
807
808 Calls `::listen(2)` then arms the io_uring multishot accept
809 operation that delivers one CQE per accepted connection.
810
811 @param impl The acceptor implementation to listen on.
812 @param backlog Maximum pending-connection queue length.
813 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
814 */
815 101x std::error_code listen_acceptor(
816 tcp_acceptor::implementation& impl, int backlog) override
817 {
818 101x auto& acc = static_cast<io_uring_tcp_acceptor&>(impl);
819 101x if (::listen(acc.fd_, backlog) < 0)
820 ✗ return make_err(errno);
821 101x acc.start_multishot();
822 101x return {};
823 }
824
825 /// Return the scheduler used by acceptors created by this service.
826 io_uring_scheduler& scheduler() noexcept { return *sched_; }
827
828 private:
829 io_uring_scheduler* sched_;
830 io_uring_tcp_service* peer_svc_;
831 std::mutex mutex_;
832 std::unordered_map<io_uring_tcp_acceptor*,
833 std::shared_ptr<io_uring_tcp_acceptor>> impls_;
834 };
835
836 /** Unix domain stream socket implementation for io_uring.
837
838 Implements `local_stream_socket::implementation` using a proactor
839 model: read, write, and connect operations are submitted to the
840 kernel via `io_uring_submit_op` and complete through the ring's
841 CQE path.
842
843 The object is always owned by a `shared_ptr` managed by the service.
844 In-flight ops hold an additional `shared_ptr` copy (`impl_ptr`) so
845 the kernel's user-data pointer remains valid until the CQE arrives.
846
847 @par Thread Safety
848 Distinct objects: Safe.
849 Shared objects: Unsafe. A socket must not have two operations of
850 the same type in flight simultaneously.
851 */
852 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_local_stream_socket final
853 : public native_socket_base<
854 io_uring_local_stream_socket,
855 local_stream_socket::implementation,
856 corosio::local_endpoint>
857 {
858 friend io_uring_local_stream_service;
859
860 io_uring_scheduler* sched_ = nullptr;
861 io_uring_local_stream_service* svc_ = nullptr;
862
863 // fd_ and local_endpoint_ live in native_socket_base, which also
864 // provides native_handle/is_open/set_option/get_option/local_endpoint.
865 corosio::local_endpoint remote_endpoint_;
866
867 // Per-fd op slots — embedded to eliminate per-call heap allocation.
868 // Single-pending invariant per slot.
869 uring_read_op rd_;
870 uring_write_op wr_;
871 uring_local_connect_op conn_;
872 uring_wait_op wait_op_;
873
874 mutable detail::speculative_state spec_;
875
876 public:
877 /** Construct with service and scheduler references.
878
879 Both refs must outlive this socket.
880
881 @param svc The owning service.
882 @param sched The io_uring scheduler owned by the context.
883 */
884 70x explicit io_uring_local_stream_socket(
885 io_uring_local_stream_service& svc,
886 io_uring_scheduler& sched) noexcept
887 140x : sched_(&sched)
888 70x , svc_(&svc)
889 70x {}
890
891 70x ~io_uring_local_stream_socket() override
892 70x {
893 70x if (fd_ >= 0)
894 ✗ ::close(fd_);
895 70x }
896
897 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
898 // io_stream::implementation
899 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
900
901 6x std::coroutine_handle<> read_some(
902 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
903 capy::executor_ref ex,
904 buffer_param buffers,
905 std::stop_token token,
906 std::error_code* ec,
907 std::size_t* bytes) override
908 {
909 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
910 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
911 6x buffers.copy_to(
912 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
913 6x io_uring_max_iov));
914 6x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
915 6x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
916
917 6x ssize_t n = 0;
918 6x int err = 0;
919 6x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
920 6x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_read())
921 {
922 5x do { n = ::readv(fd_, iovecs, iovec_count); }
923 5x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
924 5x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
925 {
926 2x have_sync_res = true;
927 2x if (n < 0) err = errno;
928 // Speculative read produced a definitive answer (data
929 // or non-EAGAIN error); reset the failure streak so a
930 // burst of past EAGAINs doesn't latch perma-off when
931 // the workload is in fact speculation-friendly.
932 2x if (n >= 0)
933 2x spec_.on_read_success();
934 }
935 else
936 {
937 3x spec_.on_read_exhausted();
938 }
939 }
940
941 6x if (have_sync_res)
942 {
943 3x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
944 {
945 3x decode_io_result(
946 ✗ ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
947 /*is_read=*/true,
948 3x n < 0 ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n), empty_buf);
949 3x if (bytes)
950 3x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
951 3x rd_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
952 3x return dispatch_coro(ex, rd_.cont_op.cont);
953 }
954 ✗ rd_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
955 ✗ shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
956 ✗ if (stop_now)
957 ✗ rd_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
958 else
959 ✗ rd_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
960 ✗ sched_->work_started();
961 {
962 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
963 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&rd_);
964 ✗ }
965 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
966 }
967
968 3x rd_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
969 6x shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
970 3x sched_->work_started();
971 3x if (rd_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
972 {
973 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
974 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&rd_);
975 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
976 ✗ }
977 3x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &rd_);
978 3x return std::noop_coroutine();
979 }
980
981 8x std::coroutine_handle<> write_some(
982 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
983 capy::executor_ref ex,
984 buffer_param buffers,
985 std::stop_token token,
986 std::error_code* ec,
987 std::size_t* bytes) override
988 {
989 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
990 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
991 8x buffers.copy_to(
992 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
993 8x io_uring_max_iov));
994 8x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
995 8x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
996
997 8x ssize_t n = 0;
998 8x int err = 0;
999 8x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
1000 8x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_write())
1001 {
1002 7x msghdr msg{};
1003 7x msg.msg_iov = iovecs;
1004 7x msg.msg_iovlen = static_cast<decltype(msg.msg_iovlen)>(iovec_count);
1005 7x do { n = ::sendmsg(fd_, &msg, MSG_NOSIGNAL); }
1006 7x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
1007 7x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
1008 {
1009 7x have_sync_res = true;
1010 7x if (n < 0) err = errno;
1011 }
1012 else
1013 {
1014 ✗ spec_.on_write_exhausted();
1015 }
1016 }
1017
1018 8x if (have_sync_res)
1019 {
1020 8x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
1021 {
1022 8x decode_io_result(
1023 ✗ ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
1024 /*is_read=*/false, /*bytes=*/0, /*empty_buffer=*/false);
1025 8x if (bytes)
1026 8x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
1027 8x wr_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
1028 8x return dispatch_coro(ex, wr_.cont_op.cont);
1029 }
1030 ✗ wr_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
1031 ✗ shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
1032 ✗ if (stop_now)
1033 ✗ wr_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
1034 else
1035 ✗ wr_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
1036 ✗ sched_->work_started();
1037 {
1038 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1039 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&wr_);
1040 ✗ }
1041 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1042 }
1043
1044 ✗ wr_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
1045 ✗ shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, token);
1046 ✗ sched_->work_started();
1047 ✗ if (wr_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1048 {
1049 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1050 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&wr_);
1051 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1052 ✗ }
1053 ✗ io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &wr_);
1054 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1055 }
1056
1057 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
1058 // local_stream_socket::implementation
1059 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
1060
1061 12x std::coroutine_handle<> connect(
1062 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1063 capy::executor_ref ex,
1064 corosio::local_endpoint ep,
1065 std::stop_token token,
1066 std::error_code* ec) override
1067 {
1068 12x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
1069 12x if (stop_now)
1070 {
1071 1x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
1072 {
1073 1x if (ec) *ec = capy::error::canceled;
1074 1x conn_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
1075 1x return dispatch_coro(ex, conn_.cont_op.cont);
1076 }
1077 ✗ conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, conn_.addr);
1078 ✗ conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
1079 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
1080 ✗ conn_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
1081 ✗ sched_->work_started();
1082 {
1083 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1084 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
1085 ✗ }
1086 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1087 }
1088
1089 // A speculative ::connect would leave the fd in EINPROGRESS and
1090 // a subsequent IORING_OP_CONNECT would see EALREADY — avoid.
1091 11x conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, conn_.addr);
1092 11x conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
1093 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
1094 11x sched_->work_started();
1095 11x if (conn_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1096 {
1097 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1098 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
1099 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1100 ✗ }
1101 11x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &conn_);
1102 11x return std::noop_coroutine();
1103 }
1104
1105 3x std::coroutine_handle<> wait(
1106 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1107 capy::executor_ref ex,
1108 wait_type w,
1109 std::stop_token token,
1110 std::error_code* ec) override
1111 {
1112 3x int poll_flags = 0;
1113 3x switch (w)
1114 {
1115 2x case wait_type::read: poll_flags = POLLIN; break;
1116 1x case wait_type::write: poll_flags = POLLOUT; break;
1117 ✗ case wait_type::error: poll_flags = POLLPRI | POLLERR | POLLHUP; break;
1118 }
1119 3x wait_op_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_,
1120 6x shared_from_this(), poll_flags, token);
1121 3x sched_->work_started();
1122 3x if (wait_op_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1123 {
1124 1x io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1125 1x sched_->push_completed_locked(&wait_op_);
1126 1x return std::noop_coroutine();
1127 1x }
1128 2x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &wait_op_);
1129 2x return std::noop_coroutine();
1130 }
1131
1132 2x std::error_code shutdown(local_stream_socket::shutdown_type what) noexcept override
1133 {
1134 2x if (::shutdown(fd_, static_cast<int>(what)) != 0)
1135 ✗ return make_err(errno);
1136 2x return {};
1137 }
1138
1139 // native_handle / is_open / set_option / get_option / local_endpoint
1140 // are inherited from native_socket_base.
1141
1142 1x native_handle_type release_socket() noexcept override
1143 {
1144 1x int fd = fd_;
1145 1x fd_ = -1;
1146 1x local_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
1147 1x remote_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
1148 1x return fd;
1149 }
1150
1151 ✗ void cancel() noexcept override
1152 {
1153 ✗ if (fd_ >= 0)
1154 ✗ sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(fd_);
1155 ✗ }
1156
1157 /// Cancel in-flight ops, close the fd, and reset cached endpoints.
1158 /// Called by the service on close()/teardown.
1159 114x void close_socket() noexcept
1160 {
1161 114x if (fd_ >= 0)
1162 {
1163 44x sched_->cancel_and_flush(fd_);
1164 44x ::close(fd_);
1165 44x fd_ = -1;
1166 }
1167 114x local_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
1168 114x remote_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
1169 114x }
1170
1171 2x corosio::local_endpoint remote_endpoint() const noexcept override
1172 {
1173 2x return remote_endpoint_;
1174 }
1175 };
1176
1177 /** Unix domain stream socket service for io_uring.
1178
1179 Owns all `io_uring_local_stream_socket` implementations for an
1180 `io_context`. Satisfies the `local_stream_service` interface so the
1181 generic `local_stream_socket` front-end can call `open_socket` and
1182 `assign_socket` transparently.
1183
1184 Socket impls are reference-counted inside the service map; raw
1185 pointers returned from `construct()` remain valid until `destroy()`
1186 or `shutdown()` is called.
1187
1188 @par Thread Safety
1189 All public member functions are thread-safe.
1190 */
1191 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_local_stream_service final
1192 : public io_uring_socket_service_base<
1193 io_uring_local_stream_service, local_stream_service,
1194 io_uring_local_stream_socket>
1195 {
1196 using base_service = io_uring_socket_service_base<
1197 io_uring_local_stream_service, local_stream_service,
1198 io_uring_local_stream_socket>;
1199
1200 public:
1201 /// Identifies this service for `execution_context` lookup.
1202 using key_type = local_stream_service;
1203
1204 /** Construct the local stream service.
1205
1206 @param ctx The owning execution context. The io_uring scheduler
1207 must already be registered.
1208 */
1209 551x explicit io_uring_local_stream_service(capy::execution_context& ctx)
1210 551x : base_service(ctx)
1211 551x {}
1212
1213 // construct / destroy / shutdown / close / scheduler() are inherited
1214 // from io_uring_socket_service_base.
1215
1216 /** Open an AF_UNIX stream socket and associate it with an impl.
1217
1218 Creates a non-blocking, close-on-exec socket via `socket(2)`.
1219 `family` is always `AF_UNIX` for local stream sockets.
1220
1221 @param impl The socket implementation to initialise.
1222 @param family Address family (`AF_UNIX`).
1223 @param type Socket type (`SOCK_STREAM`).
1224 @param protocol Protocol number (typically 0).
1225 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
1226 */
1227 20x std::error_code open_socket(
1228 local_stream_socket::implementation& impl,
1229 int family, int type, int protocol) override
1230 {
1231 20x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_socket&>(impl);
1232 20x int fd = ::socket(family, type | SOCK_NONBLOCK | SOCK_CLOEXEC, protocol);
1233 20x if (fd < 0)
1234 ✗ return make_err(errno);
1235 20x if (sock.fd_ >= 0)
1236 {
1237 ✗ sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(sock.fd_);
1238 ✗ ::close(sock.fd_);
1239 }
1240 20x sock.fd_ = fd;
1241 20x return {};
1242 }
1243
1244 /** Adopt a pre-created fd into an impl (e.g. from `socketpair`).
1245
1246 Takes ownership of `fd` on success; the caller retains ownership
1247 on failure.
1248
1249 @param impl The socket implementation to assign to.
1250 @param fd A valid, open, non-blocking AF_UNIX stream fd.
1251 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
1252 */
1253 16x std::error_code assign_socket(
1254 local_stream_socket::implementation& impl,
1255 native_handle_type fd) override
1256 {
1257 16x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_socket&>(impl);
1258 16x if (sock.fd_ >= 0)
1259 {
1260 ✗ sched_->cancel_and_flush(sock.fd_);
1261 ✗ ::close(sock.fd_);
1262 }
1263 16x sock.fd_ = static_cast<int>(fd);
1264
1265 16x sockaddr_storage local{};
1266 16x socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
1267 16x if (::getsockname(sock.fd_,
1268 16x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
1269 16x sock.local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(local, local_len);
1270
1271 16x sockaddr_storage remote{};
1272 16x socklen_t remote_len = sizeof(remote);
1273 16x if (::getpeername(sock.fd_,
1274 16x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&remote), &remote_len) == 0)
1275 16x sock.remote_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(remote, remote_len);
1276
1277 16x return {};
1278 }
1279
1280 /** Wrap an already-accepted fd as a new socket impl.
1281
1282 Called by the acceptor service after `accept(2)` returns a
1283 connected fd. Captures both endpoints via the provided peer
1284 address and a `getsockname` call.
1285
1286 @param fd Accepted file descriptor (must be non-blocking).
1287 @param peer Peer endpoint from `accept(2)`.
1288 @return Raw pointer to the registered impl.
1289 */
1290 9x io_uring_local_stream_socket* adopt_fd(
1291 int fd, corosio::local_endpoint const& peer)
1292 {
1293 9x auto p = std::make_shared<io_uring_local_stream_socket>(*this, *sched_);
1294 9x p->fd_ = fd;
1295 9x p->remote_endpoint_ = peer;
1296
1297 9x sockaddr_storage local{};
1298 9x socklen_t len = sizeof(local);
1299 9x if (::getsockname(fd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &len) == 0)
1300 9x p->local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(local, len);
1301
1302 18x return this->register_impl(std::move(p));
1303 9x }
1304 };
1305
1306 /** Local-stream (Unix domain) acceptor for io_uring.
1307
1308 Inherits all multishot machinery (parked-fd queue, waiter queue,
1309 CQE drain on destruction) from `io_uring_multishot_acceptor_base`.
1310 Adds only the `accept()` override, the `adopt_thunk` static that
1311 wraps an accepted fd via `io_uring_local_stream_service::adopt_fd`,
1312 and `release_socket()` (a pure virtual in
1313 `local_stream_acceptor::implementation` absent from the base).
1314 */
1315 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_local_stream_acceptor final
1316 : public io_uring_multishot_acceptor_base<
1317 io_uring_local_stream_acceptor,
1318 local_stream_acceptor::implementation,
1319 corosio::local_endpoint,
1320 io_uring_local_stream_service>
1321 {
1322 friend io_uring_local_stream_acceptor_service;
1323
1324 using base_type = io_uring_multishot_acceptor_base<
1325 io_uring_local_stream_acceptor,
1326 local_stream_acceptor::implementation,
1327 corosio::local_endpoint,
1328 io_uring_local_stream_service>;
1329
1330 // Readiness-wait slot. See io_uring_tcp_acceptor::wait_op_.
1331 uring_wait_op wait_op_;
1332
1333 public:
1334 27x explicit io_uring_local_stream_acceptor(
1335 io_uring_local_stream_acceptor_service&,
1336 io_uring_scheduler& sched,
1337 io_uring_local_stream_service& peer_svc) noexcept
1338 27x : base_type(sched, peer_svc)
1339 27x {}
1340
1341 13x std::coroutine_handle<> accept(
1342 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1343 capy::executor_ref ex,
1344 std::stop_token token,
1345 std::error_code* ec,
1346 io_object::implementation** impl_out) override
1347 {
1348 13x base_type::dispatch_or_queue(h, ex, token, ec, impl_out);
1349 13x return std::noop_coroutine();
1350 }
1351
1352 1x std::coroutine_handle<> wait(
1353 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1354 capy::executor_ref ex,
1355 wait_type w,
1356 std::stop_token token,
1357 std::error_code* ec) override
1358 {
1359 1x int poll_flags = 0;
1360 1x switch (w)
1361 {
1362 1x case wait_type::read: poll_flags = POLLIN; break;
1363 ✗ case wait_type::write: poll_flags = POLLOUT; break;
1364 ✗ case wait_type::error: poll_flags = POLLPRI | POLLERR | POLLHUP; break;
1365 }
1366 1x wait_op_.prepare(h, ex, ec, this->fd_, this->sched_,
1367 2x this->shared_from_this(), poll_flags, token);
1368 1x this->sched_->work_started();
1369 1x if (wait_op_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1370 {
1371 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(this->sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1372 ✗ this->sched_->push_completed_locked(&wait_op_);
1373 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1374 ✗ }
1375 1x io_uring_submit_op(*this->sched_, &wait_op_);
1376 1x return std::noop_coroutine();
1377 }
1378
1379 // release_socket() is pure virtual in local_stream_acceptor::implementation
1380 // but not in tcp_acceptor::implementation, so the base does not cover it.
1381 1x native_handle_type release_socket() noexcept override
1382 {
1383 // Mirror the service close() path: cancel the multishot SQE and
1384 // break the multi_op_ -> impl_ptr (shared_ptr<this>) cycle that
1385 // start_multishot established. Without this, the cycle keeps the
1386 // acceptor and its multi_op_ alive after the caller takes the fd,
1387 // which LeakSanitizer reports on process exit. Caller still owns
1388 // the returned fd, so we do NOT ::close it here.
1389 1x if (this->fd_ >= 0)
1390 {
1391 1x this->sched_->cancel_and_flush(this->fd_);
1392 1x this->drain_waiters_only();
1393 1x if (this->multi_op_)
1394 1x this->multi_op_->impl_ptr.reset();
1395 }
1396 1x int fd = this->fd_;
1397 1x this->fd_ = -1;
1398 1x this->local_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
1399 1x return fd;
1400 }
1401
1402 9x static io_object::implementation* adopt_thunk(
1403 void* peer_service, int fd,
1404 sockaddr_storage const& peer, socklen_t peer_len) noexcept
1405 {
1406 9x auto* svc = static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_service*>(peer_service);
1407 9x return svc->adopt_fd(fd, sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(peer, peer_len));
1408 }
1409 };
1410
1411 /** Unix domain stream acceptor service for io_uring.
1412
1413 Owns all `io_uring_local_stream_acceptor` implementations for an
1414 `io_context`. Satisfies the `local_stream_acceptor_service` interface
1415 so the generic `local_stream_acceptor` front-end can call
1416 `open_acceptor_socket`, `bind_acceptor`, and `listen_acceptor`
1417 transparently.
1418
1419 Acceptor impls are reference-counted inside the service map; raw
1420 pointers returned from `construct()` remain valid until `destroy()`
1421 or `shutdown()` is called.
1422
1423 @par Thread Safety
1424 All public member functions are thread-safe.
1425 */
1426 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_local_stream_acceptor_service final
1427 : public local_stream_acceptor_service
1428 {
1429 public:
1430 /// Identifies this service for `execution_context` lookup.
1431 using key_type = local_stream_acceptor_service;
1432
1433 /** Construct the local stream acceptor service.
1434
1435 @param ctx The owning execution context. Both the io_uring scheduler
1436 and the local stream socket service must already be registered.
1437 */
1438 551x explicit io_uring_local_stream_acceptor_service(capy::execution_context& ctx)
1439 1653x : sched_(&ctx.use_service<io_uring_scheduler>())
1440 551x , peer_svc_(&ctx.use_service<io_uring_local_stream_service>())
1441 551x {}
1442
1443 551x void shutdown() override
1444 {
1445 551x std::vector<std::shared_ptr<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor>> live;
1446 {
1447 551x std::lock_guard lk(mutex_);
1448 551x live.reserve(impls_.size());
1449 551x for (auto& [_, p] : impls_)
1450 ✗ live.push_back(p);
1451 551x }
1452 // Cancel without the lock held to avoid inversion if cancel()
1453 // re-enters the service.
1454 551x for (auto& p : live)
1455 ✗ p->cancel();
1456 551x }
1457
1458 27x io_object::implementation* construct() override
1459 {
1460 auto p = std::make_shared<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor>(
1461 27x *this, *sched_, *peer_svc_);
1462 27x auto* raw = p.get();
1463 27x std::lock_guard lk(mutex_);
1464 27x impls_.emplace(raw, std::move(p));
1465 27x return raw;
1466 27x }
1467
1468 27x void destroy(io_object::implementation* p) override
1469 {
1470 27x if (!p)
1471 ✗ return;
1472 27x std::lock_guard lk(mutex_);
1473 27x impls_.erase(static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor*>(p));
1474 27x }
1475
1476 // Close the fd eagerly when local_stream_acceptor::close() is called,
1477 // before destroy() drops the shared_ptr and the destructor runs.
1478 47x void close(io_object::handle& h) override
1479 {
1480 47x auto* acc = static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor*>(h.get());
1481 47x if (acc && acc->fd_ >= 0)
1482 {
1483 // cancel_and_flush submits cancel-by-fd; drain_waiters_only
1484 // drains queued waiters without re-submitting it.
1485 20x sched_->cancel_and_flush(acc->fd_);
1486 20x acc->drain_waiters_only();
1487 20x ::close(acc->fd_);
1488 20x acc->fd_ = -1;
1489
1490 // Break the multi_op_ -> impl_ptr (shared_ptr<this>) cycle
1491 // start_multishot established. See the symmetric comment
1492 // in io_uring_tcp_acceptor_service::close.
1493 20x if (acc->multi_op_)
1494 14x acc->multi_op_->impl_ptr.reset();
1495 }
1496 47x }
1497
1498 /** Create a non-blocking, close-on-exec AF_UNIX socket for accepting.
1499
1500 @param impl The acceptor implementation to initialise.
1501 @param family Address family (`AF_UNIX`).
1502 @param type Socket type (`SOCK_STREAM`).
1503 @param protocol Protocol number (typically 0).
1504 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
1505 */
1506 21x std::error_code open_acceptor_socket(
1507 local_stream_acceptor::implementation& impl,
1508 int family,
1509 int type,
1510 int protocol) override
1511 {
1512 21x auto& acc = static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor&>(impl);
1513 21x int fd = ::socket(family, type | SOCK_NONBLOCK | SOCK_CLOEXEC, protocol);
1514 21x if (fd < 0)
1515 ✗ return make_err(errno);
1516 21x if (acc.fd_ >= 0)
1517 {
1518 ✗ sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(acc.fd_);
1519 ✗ ::close(acc.fd_);
1520 }
1521 21x acc.fd_ = fd;
1522 21x return {};
1523 }
1524
1525 /** Bind an open acceptor and capture the local endpoint.
1526
1527 @param impl The acceptor implementation to bind.
1528 @param ep The local endpoint (path) to bind to.
1529 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
1530 */
1531 21x std::error_code bind_acceptor(
1532 local_stream_acceptor::implementation& impl,
1533 corosio::local_endpoint ep) override
1534 {
1535 21x auto& acc = static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor&>(impl);
1536 21x sockaddr_storage addr{};
1537 21x socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(ep, addr);
1538 21x if (::bind(acc.fd_, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), len) < 0)
1539 1x return make_err(errno);
1540
1541 20x sockaddr_storage local{};
1542 20x socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
1543 20x if (::getsockname(
1544 acc.fd_,
1545 20x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
1546 20x acc.local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(local, local_len);
1547 20x return {};
1548 }
1549
1550 /** Start listening and submit the multishot accept SQE.
1551
1552 Calls `::listen(2)` then arms the io_uring multishot accept
1553 operation that delivers one CQE per accepted connection.
1554
1555 @param impl The acceptor implementation to listen on.
1556 @param backlog Maximum pending-connection queue length.
1557 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
1558 */
1559 15x std::error_code listen_acceptor(
1560 local_stream_acceptor::implementation& impl,
1561 int backlog) override
1562 {
1563 15x auto& acc = static_cast<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor&>(impl);
1564 15x if (::listen(acc.fd_, backlog) < 0)
1565 ✗ return make_err(errno);
1566 15x acc.start_multishot();
1567 15x return {};
1568 }
1569
1570 /// Return the scheduler used by acceptors created by this service.
1571 io_uring_scheduler& scheduler() noexcept { return *sched_; }
1572
1573 private:
1574 io_uring_scheduler* sched_;
1575 io_uring_local_stream_service* peer_svc_;
1576 std::mutex mutex_;
1577 std::unordered_map<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor*,
1578 std::shared_ptr<io_uring_local_stream_acceptor>> impls_;
1579 };
1580
1581 /** UDP socket implementation for io_uring.
1582
1583 Implements `udp_socket::implementation` using a proactor model:
1584 send_to, recv_from, send, recv, and connect operations are submitted
1585 to the kernel via `io_uring_submit_op` and complete through the ring's
1586 CQE path.
1587
1588 The object is always owned by a `shared_ptr` managed by the service.
1589 In-flight ops hold an additional `shared_ptr` copy (`impl_ptr`) so
1590 the kernel's user-data pointer remains valid until the CQE arrives.
1591
1592 @par Thread Safety
1593 Distinct objects: Safe.
1594 Shared objects: Unsafe. One send and one recv may be in flight
1595 simultaneously, but two sends or two recvs must not overlap.
1596 */
1597 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_udp_socket final
1598 : public native_socket_base<
1599 io_uring_udp_socket, udp_socket::implementation, corosio::endpoint>
1600 {
1601 friend io_uring_udp_service;
1602
1603 int family_ = AF_UNSPEC; // cached at open_socket
1604 io_uring_scheduler* sched_ = nullptr;
1605 io_uring_udp_service* svc_ = nullptr;
1606
1607 // fd_ and local_endpoint_ live in native_socket_base, which also
1608 // provides native_handle/is_open/set_option/get_option/local_endpoint.
1609 corosio::endpoint remote_endpoint_;
1610
1611 // Per-fd op slots — embedded to eliminate per-call heap allocation.
1612 // Single-pending invariant per slot.
1613 uring_connect_op conn_;
1614 uring_dgram_send_op send_;
1615 uring_dgram_recv_op recv_;
1616 uring_wait_op wait_op_;
1617
1618 mutable detail::speculative_state spec_;
1619
1620 public:
1621 /** Construct with service and scheduler references.
1622
1623 Both refs must outlive this socket.
1624
1625 @param svc The owning service.
1626 @param sched The io_uring scheduler owned by the context.
1627 */
1628 90x explicit io_uring_udp_socket(
1629 io_uring_udp_service& svc,
1630 io_uring_scheduler& sched) noexcept
1631 180x : sched_(&sched)
1632 90x , svc_(&svc)
1633 90x {}
1634
1635 90x ~io_uring_udp_socket() override
1636 90x {
1637 90x if (fd_ >= 0)
1638 ✗ ::close(fd_);
1639 90x }
1640
1641 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
1642 // udp_socket::implementation
1643 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
1644
1645 17x std::coroutine_handle<> send_to(
1646 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1647 capy::executor_ref ex,
1648 buffer_param buf,
1649 endpoint dest,
1650 int flags,
1651 std::stop_token token,
1652 std::error_code* ec,
1653 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
1654 {
1655 17x sockaddr_storage addr{};
1656 17x socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(dest, addr);
1657 17x return submit_send(h, ex, buf, len, addr, flags,
1658 34x token, ec, bytes_out);
1659 }
1660
1661 23x std::coroutine_handle<> recv_from(
1662 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1663 capy::executor_ref ex,
1664 buffer_param buf,
1665 endpoint* source,
1666 int flags,
1667 std::stop_token token,
1668 std::error_code* ec,
1669 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
1670 {
1671 23x return submit_recv(h, ex, buf, source != nullptr, source, flags,
1672 23x token, ec, bytes_out);
1673 }
1674
1675 7x std::coroutine_handle<> send(
1676 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1677 capy::executor_ref ex,
1678 buffer_param buf,
1679 int flags,
1680 std::stop_token token,
1681 std::error_code* ec,
1682 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
1683 {
1684 7x sockaddr_storage empty{};
1685 7x return submit_send(h, ex, buf, 0, empty, flags,
1686 14x token, ec, bytes_out);
1687 }
1688
1689 5x std::coroutine_handle<> recv(
1690 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1691 capy::executor_ref ex,
1692 buffer_param buf,
1693 int flags,
1694 std::stop_token token,
1695 std::error_code* ec,
1696 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
1697 {
1698 5x return submit_recv(h, ex, buf, false, nullptr, flags,
1699 5x token, ec, bytes_out);
1700 }
1701
1702 13x std::coroutine_handle<> connect(
1703 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1704 capy::executor_ref ex,
1705 endpoint ep,
1706 std::stop_token token,
1707 std::error_code* ec) override
1708 {
1709 13x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
1710 13x if (stop_now)
1711 {
1712 1x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
1713 {
1714 1x if (ec) *ec = capy::error::canceled;
1715 1x conn_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
1716 1x return dispatch_coro(ex, conn_.cont_op.cont);
1717 }
1718 ✗ conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, family_, conn_.addr);
1719 ✗ conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
1720 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
1721 ✗ conn_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
1722 ✗ sched_->work_started();
1723 {
1724 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1725 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
1726 ✗ }
1727 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1728 }
1729
1730 // io_uring's IORING_OP_CONNECT re-invokes connect(2) internally;
1731 // a prior speculative ::connect would leave EINPROGRESS → EALREADY.
1732 12x conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, family_, conn_.addr);
1733 12x conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
1734 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
1735 12x sched_->work_started();
1736 12x if (conn_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1737 {
1738 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1739 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
1740 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1741 ✗ }
1742 12x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &conn_);
1743 12x return std::noop_coroutine();
1744 }
1745
1746 6x std::coroutine_handle<> wait(
1747 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1748 capy::executor_ref ex,
1749 wait_type w,
1750 std::stop_token token,
1751 std::error_code* ec) override
1752 {
1753 6x int poll_flags = 0;
1754 6x switch (w)
1755 {
1756 5x case wait_type::read: poll_flags = POLLIN; break;
1757 ✗ case wait_type::write: poll_flags = POLLOUT; break;
1758 1x case wait_type::error: poll_flags = POLLPRI | POLLERR | POLLHUP; break;
1759 }
1760 6x wait_op_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_,
1761 12x shared_from_this(), poll_flags, token);
1762 6x sched_->work_started();
1763 6x if (wait_op_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1764 {
1765 1x io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1766 1x sched_->push_completed_locked(&wait_op_);
1767 1x return std::noop_coroutine();
1768 1x }
1769 5x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &wait_op_);
1770 5x return std::noop_coroutine();
1771 }
1772
1773 // native_handle / is_open / set_option / get_option / local_endpoint
1774 // are inherited from native_socket_base.
1775
1776 4x void cancel() noexcept override
1777 {
1778 4x if (fd_ >= 0)
1779 4x sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(fd_);
1780 4x }
1781
1782 /// Cancel in-flight ops, close the fd, and reset cached endpoints.
1783 /// Called by the service on close()/teardown.
1784 169x void close_socket() noexcept
1785 {
1786 169x if (fd_ >= 0)
1787 {
1788 79x sched_->cancel_and_flush(fd_);
1789 79x ::close(fd_);
1790 79x fd_ = -1;
1791 }
1792 169x local_endpoint_ = endpoint{};
1793 169x remote_endpoint_ = endpoint{};
1794 169x }
1795
1796 2x endpoint remote_endpoint() const noexcept override
1797 {
1798 2x return remote_endpoint_;
1799 }
1800
1801 private:
1802 24x std::coroutine_handle<> submit_send(
1803 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1804 capy::executor_ref ex,
1805 buffer_param buffers,
1806 socklen_t dest_len,
1807 sockaddr_storage const& dest_storage,
1808 int flags,
1809 std::stop_token const& token,
1810 std::error_code* ec,
1811 std::size_t* bytes)
1812 {
1813 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
1814 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
1815 24x buffers.copy_to(
1816 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
1817 24x io_uring_max_iov));
1818 24x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
1819 24x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
1820
1821 24x ssize_t n = 0;
1822 24x int err = 0;
1823 24x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
1824 24x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_write())
1825 {
1826 21x msghdr msg{};
1827 21x msg.msg_iov = iovecs;
1828 21x msg.msg_iovlen = static_cast<decltype(msg.msg_iovlen)>(iovec_count);
1829 21x sockaddr_storage dest_copy = dest_storage;
1830 21x if (dest_len > 0)
1831 {
1832 15x msg.msg_name = &dest_copy;
1833 15x msg.msg_namelen = dest_len;
1834 }
1835 21x int native_flags = to_native_msg_flags(flags) | MSG_NOSIGNAL;
1836 21x do { n = ::sendmsg(fd_, &msg, native_flags); }
1837 21x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
1838 21x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
1839 {
1840 21x have_sync_res = true;
1841 21x if (n < 0) err = errno;
1842 }
1843 else
1844 {
1845 ✗ spec_.on_write_exhausted();
1846 }
1847 }
1848
1849 24x if (have_sync_res)
1850 {
1851 24x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
1852 {
1853 24x decode_io_result(
1854 ✗ ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
1855 /*is_read=*/false, /*bytes=*/0, /*empty_buffer=*/false);
1856 24x if (bytes)
1857 24x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
1858 24x send_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
1859 24x return dispatch_coro(ex, send_.cont_op.cont);
1860 }
1861 ✗ send_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
1862 ✗ shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, dest_len, dest_storage,
1863 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
1864 ✗ if (stop_now)
1865 ✗ send_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
1866 else
1867 ✗ send_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
1868 ✗ sched_->work_started();
1869 {
1870 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1871 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&send_);
1872 ✗ }
1873 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1874 }
1875
1876 ✗ send_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
1877 &spec_, buffers, dest_len, dest_storage,
1878 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
1879 ✗ sched_->work_started();
1880 ✗ if (send_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1881 {
1882 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1883 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&send_);
1884 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1885 ✗ }
1886 ✗ io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &send_);
1887 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1888 }
1889
1890 28x std::coroutine_handle<> submit_recv(
1891 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
1892 capy::executor_ref ex,
1893 buffer_param buffers,
1894 bool want_source,
1895 corosio::endpoint* source_out,
1896 int flags,
1897 std::stop_token const& token,
1898 std::error_code* ec,
1899 std::size_t* bytes)
1900 {
1901 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
1902 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
1903 28x buffers.copy_to(
1904 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
1905 28x io_uring_max_iov));
1906 28x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
1907 28x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
1908
1909 28x ssize_t n = 0;
1910 28x int err = 0;
1911 28x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
1912 28x sockaddr_storage src_storage{};
1913 28x socklen_t src_namelen = 0;
1914 28x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_read())
1915 {
1916 25x msghdr msg{};
1917 25x msg.msg_iov = iovecs;
1918 25x msg.msg_iovlen = static_cast<decltype(msg.msg_iovlen)>(iovec_count);
1919 25x if (want_source)
1920 {
1921 21x msg.msg_name = &src_storage;
1922 21x msg.msg_namelen = sizeof(src_storage);
1923 }
1924 25x int native_flags = to_native_msg_flags(flags);
1925 25x do { n = ::recvmsg(fd_, &msg, native_flags); }
1926 25x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
1927 25x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
1928 {
1929 16x have_sync_res = true;
1930 16x if (n < 0) err = errno;
1931 16x src_namelen = (n >= 0) ? msg.msg_namelen : 0;
1932 }
1933 else
1934 {
1935 9x spec_.on_read_exhausted();
1936 }
1937 }
1938
1939 28x if (have_sync_res)
1940 {
1941 19x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
1942 {
1943 19x decode_io_result(
1944 ✗ ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
1945 /*is_read=*/false, /*bytes=*/0, /*empty_buffer=*/false);
1946 19x if (bytes)
1947 19x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
1948 19x if (n >= 0 && want_source && source_out && !empty_buf)
1949 15x *source_out = sockaddr_to_endpoint(src_storage);
1950 19x recv_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
1951 19x return dispatch_coro(ex, recv_.cont_op.cont);
1952 }
1953 ✗ recv_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
1954 &spec_, buffers, source_out,
1955 want_source ? &write_ip_source : nullptr,
1956 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
1957 ✗ if (stop_now)
1958 ✗ recv_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
1959 else
1960 {
1961 ✗ recv_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
1962 // Hand the speculative source over to do_handler's
1963 // source_writer so it translates into source_out the same
1964 // way the kernel-completed path would.
1965 ✗ if (n >= 0 && want_source)
1966 {
1967 ✗ recv_.source_storage = src_storage;
1968 ✗ recv_.source_len = src_namelen;
1969 }
1970 }
1971 ✗ sched_->work_started();
1972 {
1973 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1974 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&recv_);
1975 ✗ }
1976 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1977 }
1978
1979 9x recv_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
1980 &spec_, buffers, source_out,
1981 want_source ? &write_ip_source : nullptr,
1982 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
1983 9x sched_->work_started();
1984 18x if (recv_.iovec_count == 0 ||
1985 9x recv_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
1986 {
1987 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
1988 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&recv_);
1989 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
1990 ✗ }
1991 9x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &recv_);
1992 9x return std::noop_coroutine();
1993 }
1994
1995 2x static void write_ip_source(
1996 void* ctx, sockaddr_storage const& s, socklen_t /*len*/) noexcept
1997 {
1998 2x if (auto* out = static_cast<corosio::endpoint*>(ctx))
1999 2x *out = sockaddr_to_endpoint(s);
2000 2x }
2001 };
2002
2003 /** UDP socket service for io_uring.
2004
2005 Owns all `io_uring_udp_socket` implementations for an `io_context`.
2006 Satisfies the `udp_service` interface so the generic `udp_socket`
2007 front-end can call `open_datagram_socket` and `bind_datagram`
2008 transparently.
2009
2010 Socket impls are reference-counted inside the service map; raw
2011 pointers returned from `construct()` remain valid until `destroy()`
2012 or `shutdown()` is called.
2013
2014 @par Thread Safety
2015 All public member functions are thread-safe.
2016 */
2017 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_udp_service final
2018 : public io_uring_socket_service_base<
2019 io_uring_udp_service, udp_service, io_uring_udp_socket>
2020 {
2021 using base_service = io_uring_socket_service_base<
2022 io_uring_udp_service, udp_service, io_uring_udp_socket>;
2023
2024 public:
2025 /// Identifies this service for `execution_context` lookup.
2026 using key_type = udp_service;
2027
2028 /** Construct the UDP service.
2029
2030 @param ctx The owning execution context. The io_uring scheduler
2031 must already be registered.
2032 */
2033 551x explicit io_uring_udp_service(capy::execution_context& ctx)
2034 551x : base_service(ctx)
2035 551x {}
2036
2037 // construct / destroy / shutdown / close / scheduler() are inherited
2038 // from io_uring_socket_service_base.
2039
2040 /** Open a datagram socket and associate it with an impl.
2041
2042 Creates a non-blocking, close-on-exec socket via `socket(2)`.
2043
2044 @param impl The socket implementation to initialise.
2045 @param family Address family (e.g. `AF_INET`, `AF_INET6`).
2046 @param type Socket type (`SOCK_DGRAM`).
2047 @param protocol Protocol number (`IPPROTO_UDP`).
2048 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
2049 */
2050 79x std::error_code open_datagram_socket(
2051 udp_socket::implementation& impl,
2052 int family, int type, int protocol) override
2053 {
2054 79x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_udp_socket&>(impl);
2055 79x int fd = ::socket(
2056 family, type | SOCK_NONBLOCK | SOCK_CLOEXEC, protocol);
2057 79x if (fd < 0)
2058 ✗ return make_err(errno);
2059 79x if (sock.fd_ >= 0)
2060 {
2061 ✗ sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(sock.fd_);
2062 ✗ ::close(sock.fd_);
2063 }
2064 79x sock.fd_ = fd;
2065 79x sock.family_ = family;
2066 79x if (family == AF_INET6)
2067 {
2068 11x int one = 1;
2069 11x ::setsockopt(fd, IPPROTO_IPV6, IPV6_V6ONLY, &one, sizeof(one));
2070 }
2071 79x return {};
2072 }
2073
2074 /** Bind the socket and capture the local endpoint via `getsockname`.
2075
2076 @param impl The socket implementation to bind.
2077 @param ep The local endpoint to bind to.
2078 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
2079 */
2080 41x std::error_code bind_datagram(
2081 udp_socket::implementation& impl, endpoint ep) override
2082 {
2083 41x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_udp_socket&>(impl);
2084 41x sockaddr_storage addr{};
2085 41x socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(ep, addr);
2086 41x if (::bind(
2087 sock.fd_,
2088 41x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), len) < 0)
2089 2x return make_err(errno);
2090
2091 39x sockaddr_storage local{};
2092 39x socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
2093 39x if (::getsockname(
2094 sock.fd_,
2095 39x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
2096 39x sock.local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_endpoint(local);
2097 39x return {};
2098 }
2099 };
2100
2101 /** Unix domain datagram socket implementation for io_uring.
2102
2103 Implements `local_datagram_socket::implementation` using a proactor
2104 model: send_to, recv_from, send, recv, and connect operations are
2105 submitted to the kernel via `io_uring_submit_op` and complete through
2106 the ring's CQE path.
2107
2108 The object is always owned by a `shared_ptr` managed by the service.
2109 In-flight ops hold an additional `shared_ptr` copy (`impl_ptr`) so
2110 the kernel's user-data pointer remains valid until the CQE arrives.
2111
2112 @par Thread Safety
2113 Distinct objects: Safe.
2114 Shared objects: Unsafe. One send and one recv may be in flight
2115 simultaneously, but two sends or two recvs must not overlap.
2116 */
2117 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_local_datagram_socket final
2118 : public native_socket_base<
2119 io_uring_local_datagram_socket,
2120 local_datagram_socket::implementation,
2121 corosio::local_endpoint>
2122 {
2123 friend io_uring_local_datagram_service;
2124
2125 io_uring_scheduler* sched_ = nullptr;
2126 io_uring_local_datagram_service* svc_ = nullptr;
2127
2128 // fd_ and local_endpoint_ live in native_socket_base, which also
2129 // provides native_handle/is_open/set_option/get_option/local_endpoint.
2130 corosio::local_endpoint remote_endpoint_;
2131
2132 // Per-fd op slots — embedded to eliminate per-call heap allocation.
2133 // Single-pending invariant per slot.
2134 uring_local_connect_op conn_;
2135 uring_dgram_send_op send_;
2136 uring_dgram_recv_op recv_;
2137 uring_wait_op wait_op_;
2138
2139 mutable detail::speculative_state spec_;
2140
2141 public:
2142 /** Construct with service and scheduler references.
2143
2144 Both refs must outlive this socket.
2145
2146 @param svc The owning service.
2147 @param sched The io_uring scheduler owned by the context.
2148 */
2149 79x explicit io_uring_local_datagram_socket(
2150 io_uring_local_datagram_service& svc,
2151 io_uring_scheduler& sched) noexcept
2152 158x : sched_(&sched)
2153 79x , svc_(&svc)
2154 79x {}
2155
2156 79x ~io_uring_local_datagram_socket() override
2157 79x {
2158 79x if (fd_ >= 0)
2159 ✗ ::close(fd_);
2160 79x }
2161
2162 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
2163 // local_datagram_socket::implementation
2164 // ----------------------------------------------------------------
2165
2166 43x std::coroutine_handle<> send_to(
2167 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2168 capy::executor_ref ex,
2169 buffer_param buf,
2170 corosio::local_endpoint dest,
2171 int flags,
2172 std::stop_token token,
2173 std::error_code* ec,
2174 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
2175 {
2176 43x sockaddr_storage addr{};
2177 43x socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(dest, addr);
2178 43x return submit_send(h, ex, buf, len, addr, flags,
2179 86x token, ec, bytes_out);
2180 }
2181
2182 41x std::coroutine_handle<> recv_from(
2183 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2184 capy::executor_ref ex,
2185 buffer_param buf,
2186 corosio::local_endpoint* source,
2187 int flags,
2188 std::stop_token token,
2189 std::error_code* ec,
2190 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
2191 {
2192 41x return submit_recv(h, ex, buf, source != nullptr, source, flags,
2193 41x token, ec, bytes_out);
2194 }
2195
2196 44x std::coroutine_handle<> send(
2197 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2198 capy::executor_ref ex,
2199 buffer_param buf,
2200 int flags,
2201 std::stop_token token,
2202 std::error_code* ec,
2203 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
2204 {
2205 44x sockaddr_storage empty{};
2206 44x return submit_send(h, ex, buf, 0, empty, flags,
2207 88x token, ec, bytes_out);
2208 }
2209
2210 43x std::coroutine_handle<> recv(
2211 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2212 capy::executor_ref ex,
2213 buffer_param buf,
2214 int flags,
2215 std::stop_token token,
2216 std::error_code* ec,
2217 std::size_t* bytes_out) override
2218 {
2219 43x return submit_recv(h, ex, buf, false, nullptr, flags,
2220 43x token, ec, bytes_out);
2221 }
2222
2223 3x std::coroutine_handle<> connect(
2224 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2225 capy::executor_ref ex,
2226 corosio::local_endpoint ep,
2227 std::stop_token token,
2228 std::error_code* ec) override
2229 {
2230 3x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
2231 3x if (stop_now)
2232 {
2233 ✗ if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
2234 {
2235 ✗ if (ec) *ec = capy::error::canceled;
2236 ✗ conn_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
2237 ✗ return dispatch_coro(ex, conn_.cont_op.cont);
2238 }
2239 ✗ conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, conn_.addr);
2240 ✗ conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
2241 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
2242 ✗ conn_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
2243 ✗ sched_->work_started();
2244 {
2245 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
2246 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
2247 ✗ }
2248 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
2249 }
2250
2251 // io_uring's IORING_OP_CONNECT re-invokes connect(2) internally;
2252 // a prior speculative ::connect would leave EINPROGRESS → EALREADY.
2253 3x conn_.addrlen = to_sockaddr(ep, conn_.addr);
2254 3x conn_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
2255 ep, &remote_endpoint_, &local_endpoint_, token);
2256 3x sched_->work_started();
2257 3x if (conn_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
2258 {
2259 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
2260 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&conn_);
2261 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
2262 ✗ }
2263 3x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &conn_);
2264 3x return std::noop_coroutine();
2265 }
2266
2267 4x std::coroutine_handle<> wait(
2268 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2269 capy::executor_ref ex,
2270 wait_type w,
2271 std::stop_token token,
2272 std::error_code* ec) override
2273 {
2274 4x int poll_flags = 0;
2275 4x switch (w)
2276 {
2277 3x case wait_type::read: poll_flags = POLLIN; break;
2278 ✗ case wait_type::write: poll_flags = POLLOUT; break;
2279 1x case wait_type::error: poll_flags = POLLPRI | POLLERR | POLLHUP; break;
2280 }
2281 4x wait_op_.prepare(h, ex, ec, fd_, sched_,
2282 8x shared_from_this(), poll_flags, token);
2283 4x sched_->work_started();
2284 4x if (wait_op_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
2285 {
2286 1x io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
2287 1x sched_->push_completed_locked(&wait_op_);
2288 1x return std::noop_coroutine();
2289 1x }
2290 3x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &wait_op_);
2291 3x return std::noop_coroutine();
2292 }
2293
2294 2x std::error_code shutdown(
2295 local_datagram_socket::shutdown_type what) noexcept override
2296 {
2297 2x if (::shutdown(fd_, static_cast<int>(what)) != 0)
2298 ✗ return make_err(errno);
2299 2x return {};
2300 }
2301
2302 // native_handle / is_open / set_option / get_option / local_endpoint
2303 // are inherited from native_socket_base.
2304
2305 1x native_handle_type release_socket() noexcept override
2306 {
2307 1x int fd = fd_;
2308 1x fd_ = -1;
2309 1x local_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
2310 1x remote_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
2311 1x return fd;
2312 }
2313
2314 1x void cancel() noexcept override
2315 {
2316 1x if (fd_ >= 0)
2317 1x sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(fd_);
2318 1x }
2319
2320 /// Cancel in-flight ops, close the fd, and reset cached endpoints.
2321 /// Called by the service on close()/teardown.
2322 145x void close_socket() noexcept
2323 {
2324 145x if (fd_ >= 0)
2325 {
2326 66x sched_->cancel_and_flush(fd_);
2327 66x ::close(fd_);
2328 66x fd_ = -1;
2329 }
2330 145x local_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
2331 145x remote_endpoint_ = corosio::local_endpoint{};
2332 145x }
2333
2334 1x corosio::local_endpoint remote_endpoint() const noexcept override
2335 {
2336 1x return remote_endpoint_;
2337 }
2338
2339 ✗ std::error_code bind(corosio::local_endpoint ep) noexcept override
2340 {
2341 ✗ sockaddr_storage addr{};
2342 ✗ socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(ep, addr);
2343 ✗ if (::bind(fd_, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), len) != 0)
2344 ✗ return make_err(errno);
2345
2346 ✗ sockaddr_storage local{};
2347 ✗ socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
2348 ✗ if (::getsockname(
2349 fd_,
2350 ✗ reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
2351 ✗ local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(local, local_len);
2352 ✗ return {};
2353 }
2354
2355 private:
2356 87x std::coroutine_handle<> submit_send(
2357 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2358 capy::executor_ref ex,
2359 buffer_param buffers,
2360 socklen_t dest_len,
2361 sockaddr_storage const& dest_storage,
2362 int flags,
2363 std::stop_token const& token,
2364 std::error_code* ec,
2365 std::size_t* bytes)
2366 {
2367 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
2368 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
2369 87x buffers.copy_to(
2370 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
2371 87x io_uring_max_iov));
2372 87x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
2373 87x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
2374
2375 87x ssize_t n = 0;
2376 87x int err = 0;
2377 87x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
2378 87x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_write())
2379 {
2380 85x msghdr msg{};
2381 85x msg.msg_iov = iovecs;
2382 85x msg.msg_iovlen = static_cast<decltype(msg.msg_iovlen)>(iovec_count);
2383 85x sockaddr_storage dest_copy = dest_storage;
2384 85x if (dest_len > 0)
2385 {
2386 42x msg.msg_name = &dest_copy;
2387 42x msg.msg_namelen = dest_len;
2388 }
2389 85x int native_flags = to_native_msg_flags(flags) | MSG_NOSIGNAL;
