2390 85x do { n = ::sendmsg(fd_, &msg, native_flags); }
2391 85x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
2392 85x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
2393 {
2394 65x have_sync_res = true;
2395 65x if (n < 0) err = errno;
2396 }
2397 else
2398 {
2399 20x spec_.on_write_exhausted();
2400 }
2401 }
2402
2403 87x if (have_sync_res)
2404 {
2405 67x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
2406 {
2407 67x decode_io_result(
2408 ✗ ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
2409 /*is_read=*/false, /*bytes=*/0, /*empty_buffer=*/false);
2410 67x if (bytes)
2411 67x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
2412 67x send_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
2413 67x return dispatch_coro(ex, send_.cont_op.cont);
2414 }
2415 ✗ send_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_,
2416 ✗ shared_from_this(), &spec_, buffers, dest_len, dest_storage,
2417 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
2418 ✗ if (stop_now)
2419 ✗ send_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
2420 else
2421 ✗ send_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
2422 ✗ sched_->work_started();
2423 {
2424 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
2425 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&send_);
2426 ✗ }
2427 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
2428 }
2429
2430 20x send_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
2431 &spec_, buffers, dest_len, dest_storage,
2432 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
2433 20x sched_->work_started();
2434 20x if (send_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
2435 {
2436 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
2437 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&send_);
2438 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
2439 ✗ }
2440 20x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &send_);
2441 20x return std::noop_coroutine();
2442 }
2443
2444 84x std::coroutine_handle<> submit_recv(
2445 std::coroutine_handle<> h,
2446 capy::executor_ref ex,
2447 buffer_param buffers,
2448 bool want_source,
2449 corosio::local_endpoint* source_out,
2450 int flags,
2451 std::stop_token const& token,
2452 std::error_code* ec,
2453 std::size_t* bytes)
2454 {
2455 iovec iovecs[io_uring_max_iov];
2456 int iovec_count = static_cast<int>(
2457 84x buffers.copy_to(
2458 reinterpret_cast<capy::mutable_buffer*>(iovecs),
2459 84x io_uring_max_iov));
2460 84x bool stop_now = token.stop_possible() && token.stop_requested();
2461 84x bool empty_buf = (iovec_count == 0);
2462
2463 84x ssize_t n = 0;
2464 84x int err = 0;
2465 84x bool have_sync_res = stop_now || empty_buf;
2466 84x sockaddr_storage src_storage{};
2467 84x socklen_t src_namelen = 0;
2468 84x if (!have_sync_res && spec_.may_speculate_read())
2469 {
2470 50x msghdr msg{};
2471 50x msg.msg_iov = iovecs;
2472 50x msg.msg_iovlen = static_cast<decltype(msg.msg_iovlen)>(iovec_count);
2473 50x if (want_source)
2474 {
2475 24x msg.msg_name = &src_storage;
2476 24x msg.msg_namelen = sizeof(src_storage);
2477 }
2478 50x int native_flags = to_native_msg_flags(flags);
2479 50x do { n = ::recvmsg(fd_, &msg, native_flags); }
2480 50x while (n < 0 && errno == EINTR);
2481 50x if (n >= 0 || (errno != EAGAIN && errno != EWOULDBLOCK))
2482 {
2483 37x have_sync_res = true;
2484 37x if (n < 0) err = errno;
2485 37x src_namelen = (n >= 0) ? msg.msg_namelen : 0;
2486 }
2487 else
2488 {
2489 13x spec_.on_read_exhausted();
2490 }
2491 }
2492
2493 84x if (have_sync_res)
2494 {
2495 39x if (sched_->try_consume_inline_budget())
2496 {
2497 39x decode_io_result(
2498 ✗ ec, stop_now, err ? make_err(err) : std::error_code{},
2499 /*is_read=*/false, /*bytes=*/0, /*empty_buffer=*/false);
2500 39x if (bytes)
2501 39x *bytes = (n < 0) ? 0u : static_cast<std::size_t>(n);
2502 39x if (n >= 0 && want_source && source_out && !empty_buf)
2503 19x *source_out = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(src_storage, src_namelen);
2504 39x recv_.cont_op.cont.h = h;
2505 39x return dispatch_coro(ex, recv_.cont_op.cont);
2506 }
2507 ✗ recv_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
2508 &spec_, buffers, source_out,
2509 want_source ? &write_local_source : nullptr,
2510 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
2511 ✗ if (stop_now)
2512 ✗ recv_.cancelled.store(true, std::memory_order_release);
2513 else
2514 {
2515 ✗ recv_.res = (n < 0) ? -err : static_cast<int>(n);
2516 // Hand the speculative source over to do_handler's
2517 // source_writer so it translates into source_out the same
2518 // way the kernel-completed path would.
2519 ✗ if (n >= 0 && want_source)
2520 {
2521 ✗ recv_.source_storage = src_storage;
2522 ✗ recv_.source_len = src_namelen;
2523 }
2524 }
2525 ✗ sched_->work_started();
2526 {
2527 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
2528 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&recv_);
2529 ✗ }
2530 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
2531 }
2532
2533 45x recv_.prepare(h, ex, ec, bytes, fd_, sched_, shared_from_this(),
2534 &spec_, buffers, source_out,
2535 want_source ? &write_local_source : nullptr,
2536 to_native_msg_flags(flags), token);
2537 45x sched_->work_started();
2538 90x if (recv_.iovec_count == 0 ||
2539 45x recv_.cancelled.load(std::memory_order_acquire))
2540 {
2541 ✗ io_uring_scheduler::lock_type lock(sched_->dispatch_mutex());
2542 ✗ sched_->push_completed_locked(&recv_);
2543 ✗ return std::noop_coroutine();
2544 ✗ }
2545 45x io_uring_submit_op(*sched_, &recv_);
2546 45x return std::noop_coroutine();
2547 }
2548
2549 21x static void write_local_source(
2550 void* ctx, sockaddr_storage const& s, socklen_t len) noexcept
2551 {
2552 21x if (auto* out = static_cast<corosio::local_endpoint*>(ctx))
2553 21x *out = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(s, len);
2554 21x }
2555 };
2556
2557 /** Unix domain datagram socket service for io_uring.
2558
2559 Owns all `io_uring_local_datagram_socket` implementations for an
2560 `io_context`. Satisfies the `local_datagram_service` interface so the
2561 generic `local_datagram_socket` front-end can call `open_socket` and
2562 `bind_socket` transparently.
2563
2564 Socket impls are reference-counted inside the service map; raw
2565 pointers returned from `construct()` remain valid until `destroy()`
2566 or `shutdown()` is called.
2567
2568 @par Thread Safety
2569 All public member functions are thread-safe.
2570 */
2571 class BOOST_COROSIO_DECL io_uring_local_datagram_service final
2572 : public io_uring_socket_service_base<
2573 io_uring_local_datagram_service, local_datagram_service,
2574 io_uring_local_datagram_socket>
2575 {
2576 using base_service = io_uring_socket_service_base<
2577 io_uring_local_datagram_service, local_datagram_service,
2578 io_uring_local_datagram_socket>;
2579
2580 public:
2581 /// Identifies this service for `execution_context` lookup.
2582 using key_type = local_datagram_service;
2583
2584 /** Construct the local datagram service.
2585
2586 @param ctx The owning execution context. The io_uring scheduler
2587 must already be registered.
2588 */
2589 551x explicit io_uring_local_datagram_service(capy::execution_context& ctx)
2590 551x : base_service(ctx)
2591 551x {}
2592
2593 // construct / destroy / shutdown / close / scheduler() are inherited
2594 // from io_uring_socket_service_base.
2595
2596 /** Open an AF_UNIX datagram socket and associate it with an impl.
2597
2598 Creates a non-blocking, close-on-exec socket via `socket(2)`.
2599 `family` is always `AF_UNIX` for local datagram sockets.
2600
2601 @param impl The socket implementation to initialise.
2602 @param family Address family (`AF_UNIX`).
2603 @param type Socket type (`SOCK_DGRAM`).
2604 @param protocol Protocol number (typically 0).
2605 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
2606 */
2607 35x std::error_code open_socket(
2608 local_datagram_socket::implementation& impl,
2609 int family, int type, int protocol) override
2610 {
2611 35x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_local_datagram_socket&>(impl);
2612 35x int fd = ::socket(family, type | SOCK_NONBLOCK | SOCK_CLOEXEC, protocol);
2613 35x if (fd < 0)
2614 ✗ return make_err(errno);
2615 35x if (sock.fd_ >= 0)
2616 {
2617 ✗ sched_->submit_cancel_by_fd(sock.fd_);
2618 ✗ ::close(sock.fd_);
2619 }
2620 35x sock.fd_ = fd;
2621 35x return {};
2622 }
2623
2624 /** Adopt a pre-created fd into an impl (e.g. from `socketpair`).
2625
2626 Takes ownership of `fd` on success; the caller retains ownership
2627 on failure.
2628
2629 @param impl The socket implementation to assign to.
2630 @param fd A valid, open, non-blocking AF_UNIX datagram fd.
2631 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
2632 */
2633 32x std::error_code assign_socket(
2634 local_datagram_socket::implementation& impl,
2635 native_handle_type fd) override
2636 {
2637 32x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_local_datagram_socket&>(impl);
2638 32x if (sock.fd_ >= 0)
2639 {
2640 ✗ sched_->cancel_and_flush(sock.fd_);
2641 ✗ ::close(sock.fd_);
2642 }
2643 32x sock.fd_ = static_cast<int>(fd);
2644
2645 32x sockaddr_storage local{};
2646 32x socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
2647 32x if (::getsockname(sock.fd_,
2648 32x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
2649 32x sock.local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(local, local_len);
2650
2651 32x sockaddr_storage remote{};
2652 32x socklen_t remote_len = sizeof(remote);
2653 32x if (::getpeername(sock.fd_,
2654 32x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&remote), &remote_len) == 0)
2655 32x sock.remote_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(remote, remote_len);
2656
2657 32x return {};
2658 }
2659
2660 /** Bind the socket and capture the local endpoint via `getsockname`.
2661
2662 @param impl The socket implementation to bind.
2663 @param ep The local endpoint (path) to bind to.
2664 @return Error code on failure, empty on success.
2665 */
2666 25x std::error_code bind_socket(
2667 local_datagram_socket::implementation& impl,
2668 corosio::local_endpoint ep) override
2669 {
2670 25x auto& sock = static_cast<io_uring_local_datagram_socket&>(impl);
2671 25x sockaddr_storage addr{};
2672 25x socklen_t len = endpoint_to_sockaddr(ep, addr);
2673 25x if (::bind(
2674 sock.fd_,
2675 25x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), len) < 0)
2676 1x return make_err(errno);
2677
2678 24x sockaddr_storage local{};
2679 24x socklen_t local_len = sizeof(local);
2680 24x if (::getsockname(
2681 sock.fd_,
2682 24x reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&local), &local_len) == 0)
2683 24x sock.local_endpoint_ = sockaddr_to_local_endpoint(local, local_len);
2684 24x return {};
2685 }
2686 };
2687
2688 } // namespace boost::corosio::detail
2689
2690 #endif // BOOST_COROSIO_HAS_IO_URING
2691
2692 #endif // BOOST_COROSIO_NATIVE_DETAIL_IO_URING_IO_URING_TYPES_HPP
